description_long: This is a twin-engine, fixed-wing airplane with a straight, high-mounted wing configuration and a conventional fuselage. The engines are mounted on the upper rear section of the fuselage, each housed in a nacelle, and the aircraft features a T-tail design with a horizontal stabilizer mounted atop the vertical fin. The landing gear is tricycle type, with two main gear units under the wings and a single nose gear. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed with multiple windows, suggesting a multi-seat arrangement. The exterior is smooth and streamlined, with minimal surface detailing and a predominantly light gray coloration. The overall design is functional and typical of small to medium-sized utility or commuter aircraft.
description: A twin-engine, high-wing airplane with a T-tail and tricycle landing gear, featuring a forward, enclosed cockpit and a streamlined, light gray exterior.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the straight, high-mounted wings, T-tail, and engine nacelles on the rear fuselage.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the cockpit windows, engine placement above the rear fuselage, and the tricycle landing gear.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the nose, cockpit, wing span, and the symmetrical layout of the landing gear.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the opposite engine nacelle, cockpit, and landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines mounted on the upper rear fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: T-tail configuration with a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer mounted at the top.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose gear, all fixed and visible.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
